capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an identity management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise identity management service exists

questions.prompt: Is the identity management service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document KPIs and KRIs for the identity management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether identity management KPIs and KRIs are documented

questions.prompt: Are identity management KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Do KPIs track orphaned or stale identities (accounts without a valid owner or recent use)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether orphaned identity metrics are measured

questions.prompt: Do KPIs include mean time to deprovision identities after role change, transfer, or termination?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether deprovisioning velocity is measured
questions.evaluation_focus: Mean time to deprovision after role change or termination

questions.prompt: Do KPIs cover machine and workload identity inventory (service principals, automation accounts, non-human identities)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether machine identity coverage is measured
questions.evaluation_focus: Machine and workload identity inventory metrics

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a centralized management server for managing identities?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a centralized identity store exists

questions.prompt: Does the identity store manage users for the entire enterprise and subsidiaries, or only enterprise employees (FTE)?
questions.intent: Evaluate enterprise-wide vs FTE-only user scope

questions.prompt: Does the identity store manage third-party and vendor users?
questions.intent: Evaluate third-party user management in the identity store

questions.prompt: Does the identity store support automation when creating subject profiles?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated subject profile creation in the identity store

questions.prompt: Is identity store configuration reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether identity store configuration is reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Do enterprise enforcement points integrate with the centralized identity store for user and identity information?
questions.intent: Evaluate enforcement point integration with the identity store
questions.evaluation_focus: Enforcement point integration with identity store

questions.prompt: Are user and identity attribute synchronizations automated with protocols like SCIM?
questions.intent: Evaluate SCIM or equivalent automation for identity data

questions.prompt: Does the identity store integrate with cloud identity providers and support directory federation (for example Azure AD, Okta, or LDAP cross-domain)?
questions.intent: Evaluate cloud IdP and federation support
questions.evaluation_focus: Cloud identity provider and directory federation

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have separate roles for management of the identity store and management of the identities themselves?
questions.intent: Evaluate separation between identity store administration and identity lifecycle roles
questions.evaluation_focus: Separate roles for identity store and identity management

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized process for generating subject profiles by considering attributes like environment, context, and requested scope?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized subject profile creation

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized process for generating object profiles by considering attributes like environment, context, and requested scope?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized object profile creation

questions.prompt: Is the subject profile process relevant across the entire enterprise, subsidiaries, and third parties—or FTE only?
questions.intent: Evaluate subject profile scope

questions.prompt: Is the object profile process relevant across the entire enterprise and third-party assets—or enterprise assets only?
questions.intent: Evaluate object profile scope

questions.prompt: Are subject and object profile creation processes automated (including non-user subjects like IoT devices and workloads)?
questions.intent: Evaluate automation of profile creation
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated subject and object profile creation

questions.prompt: Are subject and object profile creation processes reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular review of profile creation processes
questions.evaluation_focus: Subject and object profile creation review

questions.prompt: Do enterprise enforcement points incorporate subject and object profiles into policy creation?
questions.intent: Evaluate use of profiles in access policy
questions.evaluation_focus: Enforcement points use profiles in policy

questions.prompt: After profile creation, does the enterprise have a formalized documented process for provisioning identities based on profiles?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized identity provisioning process

questions.prompt: Based on triggers like compromise, role change, or identity expiry, does the enterprise have a formalized documented deprovisioning process?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized identity deprovisioning process

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise automate identity provisioning based on profiles (for example HR-driven joiner workflows)?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated identity provisioning

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise automate identity deprovisioning based on triggers (for example termination or inactivity)?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated identity deprovisioning

questions.prompt: Are provisioning and deprovisioning processes reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular review of identity lifecycle processes

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a documented formalized process for provisioning third-party users?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized third-party provisioning

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a documented formalized process for deprovisioning third-party users?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized third-party deprovisioning

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise automate third-party identity provisioning and deprovisioning based on profile and contract triggers?
questions.intent: Evaluate automation of third-party identity lifecycle

questions.prompt: Are third-party identity lifecycle processes reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular review of third-party identity processes

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized process for provisioning machine and workload identities (service principals, automation accounts, API clients)?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized machine identity provisioning
questions.evaluation_focus: Formalized machine and workload identity provisioning

questions.prompt: Are machine identities automatically deprovisioned when workloads are retired or applications are decommissioned?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated machine identity deprovisioning
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated machine identity deprovisioning on workload retirement

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise maintain an inventory of machine and workload identities with clear ownership attribution?
questions.intent: Evaluate machine identity inventory and ownership
questions.evaluation_focus: Machine identity inventory and ownership attribution

questions.prompt: Are automation and AI agent accounts governed as first-class identities in the identity store—not shared human accounts?
questions.intent: Evaluate governance of automation and AI agent identities
questions.evaluation_focus: Governance of automation and AI agent identities as first-class subjects

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an emergency break glass process when the identity store is not reachable?
questions.intent: Evaluate existence of break glass process

questions.prompt: Is the break glass process reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular break glass process review

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging identity management and lifecycle changes to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented identity lifecycle logging to SOC
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented identity lifecycle logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Is logging of identity changes and lifecycle events made securely to the SOC? (This is distinct from authentication logs or credential creation logs.)
questions.intent: Evaluate secure identity lifecycle logging
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure identity lifecycle logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are dynamic changes to identity profiles captured and transferred to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate capture of dynamic identity profile changes
questions.evaluation_focus: Dynamic identity profile changes to SOC

questions.prompt: Does the SOC detect anomalous identity lifecycle changes (for example bulk provisioning or privilege attribute modifications)?
questions.intent: Evaluate anomaly detection for identity lifecycle events
